---
title: "Cross-family bivariate models: development note"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Cross-family bivariate models: development note}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

<style>
@media (max-width: 575.98px) {
  main h1 {
    hyphens: none;
    overflow-wrap: normal;
    word-break: normal;
  }

  main table {
    display: block;
    max-width: 100%;
    overflow-x: auto;
  }
}
</style>

Cross-family bivariate models are a development route for a later release, not
a `drmTMB` 0.6.0 analysis workflow. The native TMB engine supports the released
one-response families and bivariate Gaussian models; use it for analysis and
reporting.

The implemented post-0.6 development route is a post-fit association object,
not a new joint TMB family and not a Julia bridge. It takes two already-fitted
margins on the same externally constructed complete paired rows, freezes their
stage-1 estimates, and then estimates a latent-normal association. The first
bounded pair is fixed-effect Gaussian × literal-Bernoulli, with an intercept-only
association predictor and a point estimate plus diagnostics only. This
association is `eta`, not the native bivariate-Gaussian residual correlation
`rho12`, an observed-scale correlation, or the `corpairs()` extractor (which
is distinct from the `corpair()` formula marker).

The Julia bridge remains halted/deferred and does not make Julia, JuliaCall, or
DRM.jl a package dependency. The post-fit route is not a released 0.6.0
analysis surface, and it is un-smoked: it supplies no recovery,
interval-inference, coverage, or capability claim. The deferred bridge is also
not available for 0.6.0 analysis.

For the released bivariate workflow, see
[Changing residual coupling with `rho12`](bivariate-coscale.html). For the
deferred Julia boundary, see [Julia engine: future support](julia-engine.html);
keep `engine = "tmb"` as the default.

## Development boundary

The following syntax shows the implemented development interface. It is not a
supported 0.6.0 fitting or reporting recipe. Targeted tests exist, but a smoke
and all stronger claims remain separately gated.

```r
# Post-0.6 development interface: do not use for 0.6.0 release analysis.
# paired_complete_data must be the same complete rows for both margins;
# input order may be reversed.
# fit_gaussian <- drmTMB(y_continuous ~ x, family = gaussian(),
#                        data = paired_complete_data)
# fit_bernoulli <- drmTMB(y_binary ~ x, family = stats::binomial(),
#                         data = paired_complete_data)
# assoc <- associate_pairs(
#   fit_gaussian, fit_bernoulli,
#   kernel = latent_normal(), association = ~ 1
# )
```

The first object rejects binomial trial counts, non-literal binary
values, mismatched or partial rows, weights, offsets, `mi()`, `meta_V()`, REML,
random/phylogenetic/structured effects, and association slopes. It must not
refit either margin. Standard errors, intervals, profiles, `vcov()`, residuals,
quantiles, and `emmeans` are also outside the first slice. A smoke, recovery
campaign, and any interval, coverage, or capability promotion each require a
separate owner decision; none is implied by this development implementation.

The 0.6.0 capability ledger and release scope manifest are the source of truth
for any later promotion decision.
